run1: The MrOS study commenced between 2000 and 2002. Index fractures occurred between 2000-2002 and 2019. Muscle strength and performance measurements were taken at clinical visits (2000-2002, 2005-2006, 2007-2009, 2014-2016), with index measurements taken within 5 years prior to the index fracture. Follow-up for subsequent fracture and mortality ended August 6, 2019.

run2: Patients >=50 years of age with acute back pain and vertebral fracture, matched with controls without vertebral fracture.

run1: Physical performance measurements were excluded due to acute fracture and back pain in patients, potentially affecting the full EWGSOP diagnosis.

Diagnosed according to EWGSOP criteria: decreased muscle mass with decreased muscle strength. Muscle mass was assessed using Total Psoas Area (TPA) or Psoas Lumbar Vertebral Index (PLVI) on MRI, defined as 2 standard deviations below the mean of a young adult reference population. Muscle strength was measured using a hydraulic handheld dynamometer (grip strength of dominant hand, average of three measurements), with AWGS cut-off values (26 kg for males, 18 kg for females). Physical performance was excluded due to acute fracture and back pain.
run2: EWGSOP criteria: decreased muscle mass (TPA/PLVI) with decreased muscle strength (handheld dynamometer). Cutoffs: 2 SD below the mean of a young reference population (20-40 years).

run1: Defined by European Working Group on Sarcopenia in Older People (EWGSOP) guidelines. Sarcopenia: low appendicular lean mass/height² (ALMI) AND low muscle strength. Low ALMI: <7.26 kg/m² for men, <5.5 kg/m² for women. Low strength (handgrip): <30 kg for men, <20 kg for women. Presarcopenia: only low ALMI.
run2: EWGSOP criteria: low appendicular lean mass/height^2 (ALMI < 7.26 kg/m2 in men or < 5.5 kg/m2 in women) AND low muscle strength (< 30 kg in men or < 20 kg in women).

run1: Morphometric vertebral fractures (T4-L4) defined by Genant method. Fractures were defined as scores > 1 (moderate deformity: 26-40% height reduction; severe deformity: >40% height reduction).
run2: Vertebral fractures defined by lateral X-ray using the Genant method; scores > 1 (mild, moderate, or severe deformity) were considered fractures.

run1: conservative (bracing, anti-osteoporotic and pain relief medication)
6 months for severe vertebral compression, delayed union, new OVF, and remaining LBP.

The study did not define sarcopenia. It measured paravertebral muscle (PVM) cross-sectional area (CSA), relative CSA (rCSA), and fat infiltration percentage (FI%) using MRI. No specific cutoffs for sarcopenia were provided, only measurements of PVM characteristics.
run2: Not applicable (study focused on paravertebral muscle fat infiltration, not clinical sarcopenia diagnosis)

run2: Osteoporotic vertebral fracture (OVF); severe vertebral compression defined as >40% height loss

run2: AWGS criteria: low handgrip strength (<26 kg men, <18 kg women), low gait speed (<0.8 m/sec), and low SMI (<7.0 kg/m2 men, <5.7 kg/m2 women).

run1: Elderly Chinese men and women (65-94 years) with a history of fragility fractures (ankle, wrist, vertebrae, hip) and healthy controls without fractures.
run2: Elderly men and women aged 65-94 years with fragility fractures (wrist, vertebral, hip, ankle) and healthy controls.

run1: Sarcopenia was based on appendicular lean mass (ALM/kg^2, likely a typo for ALM/height^2) measurements. The cutoff values to define class 1 and 2 sarcopenia in each gender were respectively defined as one and two standard deviations (SD) below the sex-specific means of the reference data for young people aged 18 -40 years in their previous study. Class 1 sarcopenia was skeletal muscle mass index (SMI) below 7.01 kg/m^2 in Chinese men and 5.42 kg/m^2 in Chinese women. Class 2 sarcopenia was SMI below 6.08 kg/m^2 in Chinese men and 4.79 kg/m^2 in Chinese women.
run2: SMI less than two standard deviations below the mean of a young reference group (18-40 years). Class 1: <1 SD; Class 2: <2 SD. Cutoffs: Men (Class 1 <7.01, Class 2 <6.08 kg/m2); Women (Class 1 <5.42, Class 2 <4.79 kg/m2).

run1: study-specific
DXA, BIA, dynamometer, functional_test

Sarcopenia itself was not defined as a standalone condition. However, 'low lean mass', 'low grip strength', and 'low timed get-up-and-go performance' were components of the 'Dysmobility syndrome'. Cutpoints for these components were determined by receiver-operating characteristics (ROC) analysis using the Youden index.
run2: Not applicable (Study uses 'Dysmobility syndrome' defined as ≥ 3 components among: falls, low lean mass, high fat mass, osteoporosis, low grip strength, and low timed get-up-and-go performance)

run1: mass+strength+performance

Not applicable, the study analyzed predicted body composition indices (pLBMI, pASMI, pBFMI) by quintiles, rather than defining sarcopenia with specific cutoffs. The indices were calculated using anthropometric prediction equations.
run2: Not applicable; the study evaluated body composition indices (pLBMI, pASMI, pBFMI) rather than diagnosing sarcopenia.

Participants registered for national health examinations from 2009 to 2010. Follow-up data from 1 January 2011 to 31 December 2013.

Osteoporotic fractures defined by ICD-10 codes: S22.0 (thoracic spine fracture), S22.1 (multiple thoracic spine fractures), S32.0 (lumbar spine fracture), M48.4 (fatigue fracture of a vertebra), M48.5 (collapsed vertebra) for spine fracture; S72.0 (femur neck fracture), S72.1 (trochanteric fracture), S52.5 (distal radius fracture), S52.6 (combined distal ulnar and radius fracture), S42.2 (proximal humerus fracture), S42.3 (fractured shaft of the humerus) for non-spine fracture. No specific grading method stated.

run1: individual_components_only

Patients screened from January 1, 2020, to December 31, 2020. Follow-up imaging records for at least two years were required. Imminent fracture was defined as a new or worsening fracture within 24 months after the initial vertebral fracture. Median follow-up for imminent fracture group: 9.5 months (IQR: 5-13.75 months). Median follow-up for control group: 27 months (IQR: 25-30 months).

Thoracolumbar compression fractures, fragility fractures. Imminent fracture defined as new or worsening vertebral fracture (including new vertebral fractures and progressive collapse of original vertebral body). No specific grading method stated.
run2: New or worsening fracture within 24 months after initial vertebral fracture

run1: SDOC, EWGSOP2
Low numbers of older adults with neither condition or sarcopenia alone prohibited inclusion in the model due to inadequate statistical power.
multiple

Defined by SDOC (low grip strength: < 35.5 kg for men and < 20 kg for women; slow gait speed: < 0.8 m/s) or EWGSOP2 (low grip strength: < 27 kg for men and < 16 kg for women; low ALM/h²: < 7.0 kg/m² for men and < 5.5 kg/m² for women; slow gait speed: ≤ 0.8 m/s). All three severities of sarcopenia (probable, confirmed, and severe) were classified as sarcopenic.

run1: Significant exclusions (568 for jumping mechanography due to equipment introduction, 83 unable to perform test) may introduce selection bias.
BIA, jumping_mechanography

Sarcopenia was defined by skeletal muscle index (SMI) in the lowest quartile. Lower muscle function was defined by jump power in the lowest quartile. SMI was measured by BIA, and jump power by jumping mechanography.
run2: Study-specific: lowest quartile of skeletal muscle index (SMI) for muscle mass and lowest quartile of jump power for muscle strength/function.

run1: Patients who underwent surgery for an osteoporotic vertebral compression fracture (OVCF).
run2: Patients who underwent surgery for an osteoporotic vertebral compression fracture (OVCF)

run1: Informed consent was not obtained due to the retrospective nature of the study.
1 year for the occurrence of adjacent vertebral fractures.
Multifidus muscle atrophy was assessed by cross-sectional area (mm^2) and fatty degeneration was graded using the Goutallier classification on preoperative MRI or CT scans.
individual_components_only

Retrospective study. Patients were followed for one year after the index fracture. The mean time interval between surgery of the index fracture and diagnosis of a secondary adjacent fracture was 12 weeks (SD 12, range 1-42 weeks).

Osteoporotic vertebral compression fracture (OVCF). Index fractures were classified by OF type (1-4). Adjacent osteoporotic vertebral fractures were defined as radiographically proven symptomatic fractures within two levels of the index vertebra.
run2: Symptomatic adjacent osteoporotic vertebral fracture within two levels of the index vertebra

Sarcopenia was defined according to the Japan Society of Hepatology (JSH) criteria, Asian Working Group for Sarcopenia (AWGS) criteria, and European Working Group on Sarcopenia in Older People (EWGSOP2) criteria. Skeletal muscle mass index (SMI) was assessed using bioelectrical impedance analysis (BIA) and handgrip strength using a digital grip strength dynamometer. Specific cutoffs for each guideline were not explicitly stated in the article but were implied to be those of the respective guidelines.

8 years for new vertebral fractures.
BIA, dynamometer, functional_test, gait_test

The study did not use a standard sarcopenia definition. It developed a custom scoring system for physical function (PF) and body composition (BC) to predict VFx. The PF score (0-12 points) was based on grip strength, single-leg stance, timed up-and-go, and 30-s chair stand tests. The BC score (0-6 points) was based on bioelectrical impedance measurements of trunk and appendage muscle volume. Optimal cutoffs were PF score ≥ 8 and combined PF+BC score ≥ 9.
run2: Not explicitly defined as sarcopenia; the study used a custom scoring system for physical function (grip strength, single-leg stance, timed up-and-go, 30-s chair stand) and body composition (BIA-measured muscle volume).

run1: individual_components_only
Baseline recruitment 2010-2012. Follow-up visits 2012-2015, 2015-2018, 2017-2020, 2020-2022. Study period 2010-2022.

Incident major osteoporotic fractures (MOF), vertebral fractures (VF), and non-VF (hip, humerus, forearm). MOF defined as a low-trauma VF (Grades II and III), hip, forearm, and proximal humerus fracture. Two Grade I VF were also considered as one Grade II. VF assessed by DXA-derived lateral image of the spine and graded with the semiquantitative Genant method.
run2: Major osteoporotic fractures (MOF) defined as low-trauma VF (Grades II and III), hip, forearm, and proximal humerus fracture. VF graded by semiquantitative Genant method.

explanation: The 'or' sheet contains two identical sets of multivariable logistic regression results. Furthermore, the 'Covariates' field is incorrectly or incompletely specified in both sets. The article states that the logistic regression model was 'adjusted for LSBMD and FNBMD' and presents ORs for Age, SMI, and Fat%. This implies that for each predictor (Age, SMI, or Fat%), the OR is adjusted for the other two predictors *and* for LSBMD and FNBMD.
suggested_fix: Remove one of the redundant sets of ORs. For the remaining set, update the 'Covariates' field for each multivariable logistic regression entry. For an OR of 'Age', 'Covariates' should be 'SMI, Fat%, LSBMD, FNBMD'. Similarly, for 'SMI', it should be 'Age, Fat%, LSBMD, FNBMD', and for 'Fat%', it should be 'Age, SMI, LSBMD, FNBMD'.

field_or_row: Second set of prevalence data (Outcome: Sarcopenia, Exposure: Fracture type)
explanation: The second set of prevalence data, where 'Outcome' is 'Sarcopenia' and 'Exposure' is a fracture type (e.g., 'Wrist fracture (Female)'), is a redundant re-framing of the first set. While the counts (Cases_Exp, Cases_Unexp, Controls_Exp, Controls_Unexp) are consistent with a swapped perspective, the 'Total_Cases' and 'Total_Controls' fields are misclassified if 'Sarcopenia' is considered the outcome. For example, 'Total_Cases' should represent the total number of sarcopenic individuals, not the total number of individuals in the exposure group.
suggested_fix: Remove the second set of prevalence data (all entries where 'Outcome' is 'Sarcopenia' and 'Exposure' is a fracture type). The first set correctly captures the prevalence of sarcopenia/obesity/OSO within fracture groups compared to non-fracture controls.

field_or_row: All existing rows in the 'prevalence' sheet
explanation: There is a systemic misclassification and incorrect population of fields across all existing rows in the 'prevalence' sheet. The 'Outcome' and 'Exposure' fields are consistently swapped (e.g., 'Male' is listed as 'Outcome' and 'Sarcopenia' as 'Exposure', when it should be 'Sarcopenia' as 'Outcome' and 'Male' as 'Exposure'). The 'Prevalence_pct' field also often reflects the proportion of the characteristic within the groups (e.g., % males in sarcopenia group) rather than the prevalence of the condition by characteristic (e.g., prevalence of sarcopenia in males vs females).
suggested_fix: Re-evaluate all existing rows in the 'prevalence' sheet. For each row, swap the 'Outcome' and 'Exposure' fields. Recalculate 'Cases_Exp', 'Cases_Unexp', 'Controls_Exp', 'Controls_Unexp', 'Total_Cases', and 'Total_Controls' based on the correct 2x2 table interpretation (e.g., if 'Outcome' is Sarcopenia and 'Exposure' is Male: Cases_Exp = Sarcopenia & Male, Cases_Unexp = Sarcopenia & Female, Controls_Exp = Non-sarcopenia & Male, Controls_Unexp = Non-sarcopenia & Female). Adjust 'Prevalence_pct' to reflect the prevalence of the Outcome by Exposure status, if available in the text (e.g., '28.9% (26/90) in males vs 42.3% (22/52) in females' for sarcopenia by sex).
